California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Sarah Clarenbach 
Attorney at Law 
8048 Soquel Drive 
Aptos Village, CA 
95003 
Dear Ms. Clarenbach: 
August 5, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Assistance 
Our File No. A-87-l99 
You have written to us regarding the application of the 
Political Reform Act to your client, the Aptos Incorporation 
Study Group. 
Coincidentally, a very similar letter about the 
same issues has also been received from Mr. Tom Carmody of the 
Aptos Incorporation Study Group. 
A copy of our response to his 
letter is enclosed. 
I trust that this letter, together with a 
copy of the Fontana Opinion, 2 FPPC Ops. 25 (No. 75-162, 
February 4, 1976), will satisfactorily respond to your 
questions. 
If you have any further questions or need further 
assistance, please do not hesitate to contact this office. 
I 
may be reached by phone at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
r 
I 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916) 322 .. 5660 


SARA CLARENBACH 
ATTORNEY AT LAW' ABOGADA 
8048 SOOL;EL DR!ve 
Servicios Legales Bilingues 
Ingles-Espano! 
APTOS VILLAGE, CALIFORNIA 95003 
July 16, 1987 
// 
viiane Griffiths, Esq., Chief Counsel 
Robert Leidigh, Esq., Senior Attorney 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Aptos Incorporation Study Group 
My File No. 0273 
Dear Diane, Bob, or other Attorney: 
I write pursuant to my telephone conversation of July 8 with 
Diane and July 9 with Bob regarding the above matter. 
As I told 
you, I am working with the Aptos Incorporation Study Group, and 
am making this inquiry at its request. 
The Group is a loose 
affiliation of people in the Aptos area, with no formal structure 
at this point, which is studying the feasibility of incorporating 
the community of Aptos. 
I am writing to request an opinion from your office as to 
whether the Aptos Incorporation Study Group is an entity which 
falls under the regulation of the Fair Political Practices Act. 
In that regard, what if any rules pursuant to the Act might apply 
to this Group? 
At this point, as I have indicated, the purpose of the Group 
is simply to study the feasibility of incorporation, not to take 
a position pro or con on incorporation. 
The Group wants to 
collect money from various individuals in our area with the hope 
of hiring a consultant to perform a study on feasibility of 
incorporation. It is onceivable that, if the study c mes back 
favorable tine rporation, 
some 
f 
the pe 
Ie who a e now 
working with the loose Aptos Inc rporation Study 
roup may decide 
to support incorporation. 
At that point, whichever persons from 
the Group, or persons who may not now be involved with the Group, 
d cide 
s 
p 
rt 
r ti n 
would 
r bab 
ulat 
t i e  
r 


Diane Grittiths, Esq. 
Robert Leidigh, Esq. 
July 16, 1987 
Page Two 
A question of immediate concern is whether the Group can 
start collecting money and putting it into a bank account so that 
it can talk to consultants to get proposals for a feasibility 
study. 
If you could render a quick opinion on that question to 
begin with, we would much appreciate it, so that we can either 
begin collecting money or do the proper paperwork to qualify the 
Group to do so. 
Please let me know if you need further factual background in 
order to begin your work. 
As I understand it from Bob, there is 
no charge for your service. 
If there is a fee for your work, 
please let me know that before you begin any tasks. 
I look 
forward to hearing from someone soon. 
Thank you for your 
assistance. 
Sincerely, 
SARA CLARENBACH 
Attorney at Law 
SC:dvm 
cc 
Ken Fleissner 
Terry Pershall 
Vic Marani 
Tom Carmody 
Kell 
Walker 
Doug 
onnell 
*** 
Dictated but not read by Sara Clarenbach. 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Sara Clarenbach 
8048 Soquel Drive 
Aptos Village, CA 95003 
Dear Ms. Clarenbach: 
July 21, 1987 
Re: 
87-199 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on July 20, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG: jaj 
Very truly yours, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660
